name: Keyboard Shortcuts
summary: Standardized keyboard interaction management enabling consistent shortcut handling across shadow boundaries and OS-specific layouts.

name: Composite Widget
summary: Focus management and navigation protocol for complex widgets like Grids, Trees, and Menus, implementing the Roving Tabindex pattern.

name: Droplist
summary: Accessible dropdown menu supporting keyboard navigation, typeahead, and ARIA roles.

name: Validation
summary: Standard behaviors for native-like form validation in custom elements.

name: Resource Loader
summary: Structural directive using comment-based virtual elements to handle async state without polluting the DOM.

name: Resource Action
summary: Behavioral directive that attaches async lifecycle management to triggers via virtual markers.

name: For Each
summary: Structural directive for rendering lists of items based on an array.

name: Handler Expression Parser
summary: Parser for function-call style event handler expressions. Extends CustomAttributeParser for registry integration.

magicVariables.description: Magic variables are prefixed with $ to distinguish them from user-defined state

variables.$event: The native DOM Event object passed to the handler
variables.$element: The element the event handler is attached to
variables.$target: The event.target - the element that triggered the event

magicVariables.rationale: Using $ is common in Vue, Svelte, and other frameworks. It creates a clear visual distinction between framework-provided values and user data.

namedContexts.description: Named contexts are prefixed with @ to access scoped data from parent elements

examples.@state: Explicit access to the default state context

namedContexts.rationale: The @ prefix is distinct from $ and provides clear scoping. Similar to Angular's template reference variables but for contexts.

defaultState.description: Bare identifiers resolve to the default state context
defaultState.rationale: Most expressions reference state, so bare identifiers reduce verbosity. @state.count and count are equivalent.

contextShadowing.description: Controls behavior when a context name shadows another in nested scopes
contextShadowing.rationale: Shadowing can cause subtle bugs. Default to warning to alert developers while not breaking during development.

forEachNaming.description: The 'as' keyword names the loop context
forEachNaming.rationale: Matches PHP/Blade/many template languages. Intuitive for developers familiar with foreach loops.

name: Event Behaviors (on:*)
summary: Declarative event-to-action mapping using expression syntax with context resolution.

configuration.getHandlers: Required. Function returning handler registry for an element
configuration.getContexts: Optional. Function returning named contexts Map
configuration.getState: Optional. Function returning default state object
configuration.parserName: Optional. Name of parser in CustomAttributeParserRegistry (default: 'handler')

parserResolution.description: HandlerExpressionParser is resolved from CustomAttributeParserRegistry in the injector chain
parserResolution.error: MissingParserError thrown if no parser found in registry

designRationale: Parser registry allows different DOM subtrees to use different parser configurations. Parsers extend CustomAttributeParser for consistency with the attribute parsing system.

name: Trusted HTML
summary: Structural directive enforcing Trusted Types policies on dynamic HTML content via comment-based virtual elements.

name: Trusted HTML Behavior
summary: Attribute-based behavior that enforces Trusted Types policies on an element's innerHTML mutations.
